commit.message:
Fix Android Auto truncating podcast episode lists (#54)

* Initial plan

* Fix Android Auto not showing all podcast episodes

Remove the artificial 100-episode per-page cap in onLoadChildren and
the "More episodes…" FLAG_BROWSABLE item that were preventing Android
Auto from seeing the full episode list.

MediaBrowserServiceCompat.applyOptions() slices onLoadChildren results
by EXTRA_PAGE / EXTRA_PAGE_SIZE before sending them to the client.
The old approach capped results at 100 and added a "More episodes…"
item that was silently dropped by this framework slicing, making
episodes beyond page 1 inaccessible.

The fix returns ALL episodes as a flat list. The framework's applyOptions
then correctly pages through the complete list as Android Auto requests
each page. fetchEpisodesPaged(0, Int.MAX_VALUE) adds no extra network or
memory cost since the RSS feed is always fully downloaded before slicing.

Backward compatibility: the podcast ID extraction still strips any
legacy ":start=<n>:count=<m>" suffix so cached navigation items from the
old approach do not crash.

feat: make image resizing opt-in via MANAEL_ENABLE_RESIZE env var (#1647)

- [x] Update `manael_test.go`: add `WithResizeEnabled(true)` to existing
resize tests, add `TestNewServeProxy_resizeDisabled` test
- [x] Update English documentation (`usage.md`) to document

- [x] Update Japanese documentation (`ja/docs/usage.md`) to document

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Security: Make image resizing an opt-in feature (disabled
by default)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> The image resizing feature has been successfully implemented and
merged, including the necessary safeguards for dimension limits.
However, it is currently enabled by default.
> Because resizing is a highly memory- and CPU-intensive operation,
leaving it enabled by default could expose Manael to unintended resource
exhaustion if users or malicious actors send requests with resize
parameters. To adhere to a "secure-by-default" and lightweight
philosophy, this feature must be strictly opt-in.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Introduce an environment variable `MANAEL_ENABLE_RESIZE` to control
the resizing feature. By default, this feature should be disabled.
> If a client passes resize query parameters (`width` or `height`) when
the feature is disabled, Manael should simply ignore the parameters and
proceed with standard format conversion on the original-size image.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - Introduce the `MANAEL_ENABLE_RESIZE` environment variable (boolean,
default: `false` or unset).
> - Update the request parsing and handling logic to check this
variable. If it is not set to `true`, bypass the resize logic
completely.
> - Update the documentation (Deployment/Usage guides) to explain how to
enable resizing via this environment variable.
> 
> ### Example Implementation Idea
> ```go
> // Inside the handler or parsing logic
> if os.Getenv("MANAEL_ENABLE_RESIZE") != "true" {
>     // Ignore resize parameters and proceed without resizing
>     // e.g., do not pass width/height to the processing engine
> }
> ```</issue_description>

commit.message:
feat: diagram gallery & persistence (v0.5.0)

- Auto-save diagrams to .excalidraw-copilot/ after generation and refinement
- Activity Bar gallery panel with file watcher for browsing saved diagrams
- Gallery actions: open, delete, rename, reveal in Explorer, refine
- Custom editor for .excalidraw files (double-click to open)
- Refine saved diagrams via popup or @excalidraw chat
- Gallery refinement saves as -refined copy (original preserved)
- DSL stores graph JSON, Mermaid stores syntax for round-trip refinement
- Git integration: asks once to add .excalidraw-copilot/ to .gitignore
- Gallery auto-reveals on first save with one-time notification
- Filename format: name-first ({slug}_{timestamp}.excalidraw)
- Version bumped to 0.5.0

Fixed:
- Mermaid auto-save empty canvas (stores syntax directly)
- getCanvasState() race condition (queue pattern)
- Filename collisions (second-precision timestamps)
- Duplicate files on refinement (tracked URI for overwrite)
- galleryRefine double-trigger (separate from galleryOpen)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
